rev —

IBIS Model Builder

Generate a board-level .ibs model in your browser — from device geometry, from measured edge rates, or from raw I‑V/V‑t data. 100% client-side.

Estimate, not sign-off. The W/L path uses long-channel square-law physics and will overstate drive at modern nodes. Trust the Rise/Fall and Paste Data paths more than the geometry path. Always correlate against SPICE or silicon before real use.
Index
  1. 1 · Component & model
  2. 2 · Input data W/L + process · Rise/Fall · Paste I‑V · SPICE netlist · Cadence
  3. 3 · Live model preview
  4. 4 · Output .ibs

1 · Component & model

Package & pin parasitics, temperature

2 · Where does the data come from?

Edit process parameters (square-law)

Auto-detected below — override any field if the guess is wrong.

⚠ A browser can't read a Cadence OA database or a filesystem path directly. Enter your cell + pin below and this generates a Virtuoso SKILL/OCEAN script that netlists the cell and prints the pin's driver W/L. Run it in the CIW, then paste the numbers (or the netlist) back here.

        
Run the above in the Virtuoso CIW. It lists every instance on the PAD net with its W/L — your drivers are the pfet (pull-up) and nfet (pull-down) rows. If nothing prints, the net name didn't match your pattern — the script then lists the cell's actual net names so you can fix the pattern. Most reliable path: it also writes a SPICE netlist you can paste into the Paste SPICE netlist tab. Then enter the W/L below to build directly.

      

      

        
        
        
        
        
        
      

    


    
  


  
  

    
Live model preview ● updates as you type

    
Enter inputs above to see the live model diagram.

  


  
  

    
3 · Output

    

    

      
      
    

    

      Pulldown (NMOS)
      Pullup (PMOS)
      POWER clamp
      GND clamp
      C_comp
      Package R/L/C
      Ramp
    

    

  






  
Open source · MIT · github.com/borenw/ibis-model-builder. Not affiliated with the IBIS Open Forum. Validate output with ibischk before use.
